title: Meta Tag Generator
shortDescription: Build HTML meta tags for page title, description, canonical URL, and Open Graph fields.
metaDescription: Create ready-to-paste HTML meta tags for title, description, canonical URL, and Open Graph data in one place.
intro: Create the HTML meta tags your page needs: title, description, canonical URL, and Open Graph fields.
overview:
Use this tool to assemble the head metadata for a page without writing each tag by hand. It produces a structured snippet you can place in your document head, which is useful when you need consistent titles, a clear description, a canonical URL, and social preview data on the same page.

The generator is best when you are preparing a new page, updating an existing article, or standardizing metadata across a site. Enter the page details once, review the generated tags, and copy only the parts you want to use. This helps keep page metadata aligned with the content and avoids missing fields or broken attribute values.
howItWorks: Enter the page title, description, canonical URL, site name, and optional Open Graph image.
Generate the meta tag snippet from the values you provided.
Review the output and check whether each field matches the page content.
Copy the snippet into the head section of your HTML document.

useCases.title: Product page metadata
useCases.description: Create title, description, canonical, and social preview tags for a product detail page with one consistent set of values.

useCases.title: Article header tags
useCases.description: Prepare metadata for a news story, guide, or blog post so the page title and summary match the published content.

useCases.title: Duplicate page cleanup
useCases.description: Add a canonical URL to a filtered, paginated, or tracked version of a page so the preferred address is explicit.

useCases.title: Campaign landing page setup
useCases.description: Generate metadata for a launch page with a campaign title, a short benefit-focused description, and a branded preview image.

useCases.title: Social share preview control
useCases.description: Define Open Graph title, description, site name, and image so shared links display the intended message.

examples.title: Compact running backpack
examples.input:
Title: Compact running backpack
Description: Lightweight 8L pack with hydration sleeve and chest strap.
Canonical URL: https://example.com/products/compact-running-backpack
Site name: TrailForge
OG image: https://example.com/images/backpack-og.jpg
examples.output:
<title>Compact running backpack</title>
<meta name="description" content="Lightweight 8L pack with hydration sleeve and chest strap.">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/products/compact-running-backpack">
<meta property="og:site_name" content="TrailForge">
<meta property="og:title" content="Compact running backpack">
<meta property="og:description" content="Lightweight 8L pack with hydration sleeve and chest strap.">
<meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/images/backpack-og.jpg">
examples.description: Useful for a product page with a single preferred URL and a branded preview image.

examples.title: City budget guide
examples.input:
Title: 3-day budget guide for Lisbon
Description: A day-by-day trip plan with transit, food, and stay costs.
Canonical URL: https://example.com/guides/lisbon-budget
Site name: RouteNotes
examples.output:
<title>3-day budget guide for Lisbon</title>
<meta name="description" content="A day-by-day trip plan with transit, food, and stay costs.">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/guides/lisbon-budget">
<meta property="og:site_name" content="RouteNotes">
<meta property="og:title" content="3-day budget guide for Lisbon">
<meta property="og:description" content="A day-by-day trip plan with transit, food, and stay costs.">
examples.description: Handy for an editorial page where the summary needs to stay short and specific.

examples.title: Webinar registration page
examples.input:
Title: Design systems webinar
Description: Live session on tokens, components, and team workflows.
Canonical URL: https://example.com/webinars/design-systems
Site name: StudioLine
OG image: https://cdn.example.com/webinars/design-system-share.png
examples.output:
<title>Design systems webinar</title>
<meta name="description" content="Live session on tokens, components, and team workflows.">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/webinars/design-systems">
<meta property="og:site_name" content="StudioLine">
<meta property="og:title" content="Design systems webinar">
<meta property="og:description" content="Live session on tokens, components, and team workflows.">
<meta property="og:image" content="https://cdn.example.com/webinars/design-system-share.png">
examples.description: Ideal for an event page that needs a clear preview when shared.

faq.question: Which fields should I fill first if I only need the core tags?
faq.answer: Start with title, description, and canonical URL. Add site name and image only if you want the shared preview to include branding and artwork.

faq.question: Does the tool replace my page title or description automatically?
faq.answer: No. It generates the HTML tags from the values you enter. If the content changes later, regenerate the snippet with the updated text.

faq.question: Why does the canonical URL matter in the output?
faq.answer: It tells browsers and crawlers which address should be treated as the main version of the page. Use the final preferred URL, not a tracking link or staging address.

faq.question: What is the most common mistake when using the output?
faq.answer: People often paste the snippet in the wrong part of the document or reuse a description that is too long, vague, or unrelated to the actual page content.
